bikes.blurb: The best selling budget folder in America from 2022 to 2024, replaced by the XP4 in 2025. Owners swap the same four parts in month one: saddle, seatpost, pedals, tires. Takeoff supply is deep and buyer demand from budget repairers is steady.

stock_parts.comp_note: Takeoff stock ebike saddles list $10-25 on eBay and forum classifieds; no set of 3+ confirmed solds found, so priced at the low end.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select Airflow saddle
upgrade.note: First thing off the bike. The stock pad is thin and everyone feels it by mile five.

stock_parts.comp_note: Bare rigid posts barely move used; value is mostly in bundles. Priced accordingly.

upgrade.name: SP12 NCX suspension seatpost
upgrade.note: The number one XP comfort fix on every owner forum. Rigid frame plus fat tires still beats you up without it.

stock_parts.comp_note: Folding takeoff pedals sell in the $10-15 range when they sell at all. No confirmed sold set.

upgrade.name: Chester composite platform pedals
upgrade.note: Stock folders are small and slick when wet. A real platform pedal fixes footing for cheap.

stock_parts.comp_note: Used 20x3 takeoff pairs list around $30-40; single tires priced low. Lectric sells this tire new in pairs, which caps used value.

upgrade.name: Utilit Seaside 20x3.0 tire
upgrade.note: The ebikesforum pick for a tougher 20x3 that fits the stock rim and fenders.

stock_parts.stock_part: Star Union mechanical disc calipers (early 3.0 builds; later builds shipped hydraulic)
stock_parts.comp_note: Cable-pull calipers are near worthless used; Lectric moved the 3.0 to hydraulics mid-run, which killed remaining demand.

upgrade.name: HD-E350 hydraulic disc brake set
upgrade.note: Early 3.0 owners dump the cable brakes the first time they load the bike up. Hydraulics are the standard fix.

stock_parts.comp_note: New replacement displays list $50-80 on eBay; used takeoffs should clear under that. No confirmed sold set.
OEM spares list $150-250 on eBay; one aftermarket 48V 10.4Ah listing showed 13 sold near $150. Health-dependent, so priced at the low end.

upgrade.name: Long-Range 48V 14Ah battery
upgrade.note: The long-range pack is why the 10.4 comes off. Half the takeoff supply comes from range upgrades.

stock_parts.comp_note: No real used market for basic rubber grips. Bundle filler only.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Cheap grips, numb hands. GP1s are the boring right answer.

bikes.blurb: Lectric's entry folder, refreshed in 2024 with hydraulic brakes and a color display. Single speed keeps it simple. Same comfort-swap pattern as the XP: smaller parts, smaller money.

stock_parts.comp_note: Same saddle family as the XP line; takeoffs list $10-25, no confirmed sold set.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select Airflow saddle
upgrade.note: Same story as every Lectric: the stock saddle is the first casualty.

stock_parts.comp_note: Bare rigid posts barely move used. Bundle filler.

upgrade.name: SP12 NCX suspension seatpost
upgrade.note: No suspension anywhere on the Lite, so the seatpost does all the work. Standard swap.

stock_parts.comp_note: Same folding pedal as the XP line; $10-15 territory used.

upgrade.name: Chester composite platform pedals
upgrade.note: Tiny folders on a single speed you actually pedal. Riders notice fast.

stock_parts.comp_note: No real used market for basic rubber grips.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Standard comfort fix on any budget commuter.

stock_parts.stock_part: 20x2.5 slick with reflective strip and Hippo Skin liner
stock_parts.comp_note: Model-specific slick with thin used demand; priced low.

upgrade.name: Big Apple 20x2.35 tire
upgrade.note: Fits the Lite's street mission and rides softer than the stock slick.

stock_parts.comp_note: Current-generation Lectric color display shared across the 2024+ lineup; only crash replacements create demand. No sold data found.
Sparse listings for this small pack; priced low against the $150-250 asks seen for larger Lectric packs.

upgrade.name: XP Lite Long-Range 672Wh battery
upgrade.note: The 375Wh pack runs out fast. Long-range upgrades feed the takeoff supply.

bikes.motor: 750W geared rear hub (1310W peak, 85Nm, Stealth M24 on 2.0)
bikes.blurb: The budget cargo workhorse, 450 lb payload on the 2.0. Families load it up and upgrade range and rubber first. The rack is part of the frame, so takeoffs skew batteries, tires, and contact points.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared Lectric saddle family; $10-25 listings, no confirmed sold set.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select Airflow saddle
upgrade.note: Cargo miles are long miles. The stock saddle does not survive them.

stock_parts.comp_note: Bare rigid posts barely move used.

upgrade.name: SP12 NCX suspension seatpost
upgrade.note: The 1.0 has no suspension at all. This is the cheapest way to make a loaded bike ride civilized.

stock_parts.comp_note: Lectric packs list $150-250 on eBay depending on size and health; no confirmed sold set for this specific pack. Priced low.

upgrade.name: XPedition Long-Range 48V 17.5Ah battery
upgrade.note: Cargo duty eats range. Most takeoff 13Ah packs come out of dual-battery and long-range upgrades.

stock_parts.comp_note: 20-inch takeoff tires list $15-25 each; cargo wear shortens usable life, priced low.

upgrade.name: Pick-Up 20x2.6 cargo tire
upgrade.note: Cargo-rated casing. The stock rubber squirms once you put 200 pounds on the rack.

stock_parts.stock_part: Lectric color TFT display with USB (2.0)
stock_parts.comp_note: Shared 2024+ Lectric color display; crash-replacement demand only. No sold data found.
No real used market for stock rubber grips.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Long hauls with a load up front make palm support worth the money.

bikes.motor: 750W geared rear hub (1310W peak, 85Nm, Stealth M24)
bikes.blurb: Torque-sensor commuter at a throwaway price, with an SR Suntour fork and Shimano Altus 8-speed stock. Contact-point swaps dominate; the fork and mech mostly come off when a bike gets parted out.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared Lectric saddle family; $10-25 listings, no confirmed sold set.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select Airflow saddle
upgrade.note: Commuters ride daily and swap the saddle first. Same as every bike in this class.

stock_parts.comp_note: Bare rigid posts barely move used.

upgrade.name: SP12 NCX suspension seatpost
upgrade.note: Front fork alone does not save your spine on city streets. The NCX finishes the job.

stock_parts.comp_note: No real used market for stock rubber grips.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Daily-commute hands want the flat platform. Easy sell.

stock_parts.comp_note: Entry Suntour coil forks pull $40-70 used as takeoffs; mostly part-out supply, not upgrade swaps.
Shared 2024+ Lectric color display; crash-replacement demand only.
Downtube form factor is XPress-specific. Lectric packs list $150-250 on eBay; priced mid-low pending sold data.
Altus mechs sell $10-20 used everywhere; commodity part.
Generic 27.5 commuter takeoffs run $15-25 each used; no model-specific comps found.

upgrade.name: Super Moto-X 27.5x2.4 tire
upgrade.note: The standard e-rated street upgrade in this size. Faster and tougher than stock.

bikes.motor: 750W geared rear hub (1310W peak, 85Nm, Stealth M24)
bikes.blurb: Lectric's fat tire adventure bike with an RST Renegade fork and hydraulic brakes stock. Riders who use it as intended chew through tires and want more range. Lock-on grips and real suspension mean fewer comfort swaps than the XP line.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared Lectric saddle family; $10-25 listings, no confirmed sold set.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select Airflow saddle
upgrade.note: Trail miles punish a budget saddle same as street miles. Still the first swap.

stock_parts.comp_note: Bare rigid posts barely move used.

upgrade.name: SP12 NCX suspension seatpost
upgrade.note: Hardtail with 80mm up front still hammers the saddle. The NCX covers the back half.

stock_parts.comp_note: Lock-ons are cheap new; no meaningful used market.

upgrade.name: GA3 lock-on grips
upgrade.note: Stock lock-ons are fine. Riders who swap want the wing for washboard descents.

stock_parts.comp_note: Fat-bike coil forks pull $50-90 used; supply is part-outs, not upgrades. No confirmed sold set.
Used 26x4 fat takeoffs list $20-35 each; heavy to ship, which drags realized prices down.

upgrade.name: Minion FBF 26x4 tire
upgrade.note: The serious-traction swap. Stock knobbies are okay until the trail gets loose.

stock_parts.comp_note: Lectric packs list $150-250 on eBay; XPeak-specific sold data not found, priced mid-low.

upgrade.name: XPeak Long-Range 48V 20Ah battery
upgrade.note: Backcountry range anxiety sells the 960Wh pack and frees up the 15Ah takeoff.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared 2024+ Lectric color display; crash-replacement demand only.

bikes.motor: 500W mid-mounted jackshaft motor driving a rear differential (1092W peak, 65Nm)
bikes.blurb: The default budget electric trike until the Trike2 replaced it in 2025. Buyers skew older and want backrest saddles almost on day one. Hydraulic brakes, single speed, and the XP-pattern battery keep parts interchangeable and moving.

stock_parts.comp_note: Wide takeoff saddles list $10-25; steady trickle of trike-owner demand, no confirmed sold set.

upgrade.name: Tri-Cruiser saddle with backrest
upgrade.note: Trike buyers want a backrest, full stop. This is the swap nearly every mobility-minded owner makes.

stock_parts.comp_note: Same pack family as the XP long-range, which broadens the buyer pool. OEM asks run $200-280; priced under.
Shared XP-series display; crash and water damage drive replacement demand.
Low-speed trike duty means slow wear and thin swap volume; priced low.
Bolt-on baskets hold value with trike buyers who removed theirs; thin data, priced low.
No real used market for stock rubber grips.

upgrade.name: GP1 ergonomic grips
upgrade.note: Arthritic hands are half this bike's market. Ergo grips are an easy add-on sale.

stock_parts.comp_note: Same folding pedal as the XP line; $10-15 territory used.

bikes.blurb: Fast 52V commuter from a brand that collapsed into bankruptcy in 2024. Owners cannot buy OEM anything anymore, so working takeoffs, above all batteries, displays, and controllers, carry real scarcity value. Price and hold accordingly.

stock_parts.comp_note: Name-brand takeoff saddles pull $15-30 used; better than house-brand but still no confirmed sold set.

upgrade.name: Cruiser Select Airflow saddle
upgrade.note: The Selle Royal is decent, but 32 mph commuters still trade it for more padding.

stock_parts.comp_note: New-replacement listings run $520-620 since the 2024 shutdown; working used G2 packs list $250-450. Demand is real, confirmed solds are thin, so priced at the low end.

upgrade.name: G2 52V replacement pack
upgrade.note: Juiced is gone, so a dying pack means aftermarket or rebuild. Working takeoff packs move fast because of it.

stock_parts.comp_note: Aftermarket CrossCurrent/RipCurrent replacements run about $90 new at GreenBikeKit; used OEM priced under that. Dead brand keeps demand alive.

upgrade.name: KT 52V controller (CrossCurrent/RipCurrent fit)
upgrade.note: When the stock controller cooks there is no Juiced to call. The KT swap is the known path.

stock_parts.comp_note: No OEM source since the shutdown; scarce takeoffs command real money from stranded owners, but no sold set found.

stock_parts.stock_part: Bafang 750W geared rear hub laced to 27.5 wheel
stock_parts.comp_note: Bafang hub motor wheels list $120-250 on eBay new and used; laced takeoffs priced at the low end pending sold data.
Entry suspension forks pull $40-70 used; part-out supply only.
Common size with plentiful new alternatives; used takeoffs priced low.

upgrade.name: Super Moto-X 27.5x2.4 tire
upgrade.note: E-rated casing built for exactly this kind of 32 mph commuter abuse.

stock_parts.stock_part: Juiced rear rack and fender set
stock_parts.catalog_name: Juiced rear rack and fender set (CrossCurrent)
stock_parts.comp_note: Dead brand makes even bolt-on tin worth something to owners replacing bent parts; thin data, priced low.

bikes.blurb: Juiced's best seller: a 28 mph fat tire commuter with a Shimano 9-speed and hydraulic brakes. The brand's 2024 collapse turned every working takeoff into supported-life insurance for stranded owners. Batteries and controllers are the money parts.

stock_parts.comp_note: Name-brand takeoff saddles pull $15-30 used; EBR forum threads document owners swapping this one out.

upgrade.name: A.R.S. Spring saddle
upgrade.note: Owners on the EBR forum call the difference light years. The stock gel bottoms out under bigger riders.

stock_parts.comp_note: Bare rigid posts barely move used; bundle filler.

upgrade.name: SP12 NCX suspension seatpost
upgrade.note: Pairs with the saddle swap. Fat tires alone do not save your back at 28 mph.

stock_parts.comp_note: Juiced sold an official upgraded controller for this bike and forum owners installed it, creating stock takeoffs. Aftermarket replacements run about $90 new; used OEM priced under.

upgrade.name: KT 52V controller (CrossCurrent/RipCurrent fit)
upgrade.note: The factory hot-rod controller died with the company. The KT is the only game left.

stock_parts.comp_note: Same G2 pack as the CrossCurrent X. New replacements $520-620, used working packs listed $250-450; thin confirmed solds, priced low.

upgrade.name: G2 52V replacement pack
upgrade.note: Dead brand plus aging cells equals the strongest demand line in the whole Juiced catalog.

stock_parts.stock_part: Bafang 750W geared rear hub laced to 26x4 wheel
stock_parts.comp_note: EBR forum threads show owners swapping this hub for bigger motors, so takeoffs exist. Bafang hub wheels list $120-250; priced at the low end.
BFTs run $40-50 new; used takeoffs realistically $15-25 given shipping weight.

upgrade.name: Supercell 26x4 street tire
upgrade.note: Commuters ditch the knobbies for slicks and pick up real range doing it.

stock_parts.comp_note: Fat-bike air forks pull $50-90 used; dead-brand fitment questions cap the price. No sold set.
Shared across the Juiced lineup and unobtainable new since the shutdown; scarcity value, thin data.

bikes.blurb: Full-suspension 52V moped, orphaned when Juiced folded in 2024. Almost nothing on it cross-shops with standard bike parts, which cuts both ways: takeoffs are scarce and wanted, but the only buyers are other Scorpion owners.

stock_parts.comp_note: Aftermarket replacements list $520-590 on eBay since the shutdown; used OEM packs priced at half or less on health risk. No confirmed sold set.

upgrade.name: 52V G-pack rebuild service
upgrade.note: Chi rebuilds the dead pack in the original case. The only sane route since Juiced folded.

stock_parts.comp_note: Model-specific moped seat with no aftermarket equal; dead-brand scarcity supports the price, but data is thin.
Odd moped size still sold new around $50-60; heavy throttle bikes wear tires fast so demand recurs. Used priced low.
Moped triple-trees have no drop-in substitute and the brand is dead; takeoffs hold value but no sold set exists.
Generic coil-over pairs sell $30-60 used; Scorpion fitment adds a little, thin data.

upgrade.name: RCP-2S coil shocks (pair)
upgrade.note: Standard e-moped shock upgrade when the stock coils get mushy under a passenger.

stock_parts.stock_part: Juiced LCD advanced matrix display with USB
stock_parts.comp_note: Shared Juiced display, unobtainable new; scarcity value from stranded owners, no sold set.
Higher-amp unit than the CrossCurrent/RipCurrent controller; same dead-brand dynamics, priced in line with aftermarket 52V replacements near $90 new.
